entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated brain uses a self-wiring knowledge graph to automatically extract entities and relationships from raw data. It features a hybrid search system that combines vector RAG with graph-based ranking, supported by 28 specialized skills for autonomous data enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It allows Hermes to build evolving mental models and integrates seamlessly with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: Designed for complex tasks, this recursive harness uses a multi-agent loop—including roles like coach and architect—to accumulate validated knowledge over repeated runs. It ensures mission success through verifier-driven checkpoints and browser-integrated investigations.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin provides lossless context management by storing an immutable history in a hierarchical DAG structure within SQLite. It uses a three-level escalation system for context compaction, allowing agents to grep or expand history without ever deleting original message data.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project offers a private, self-hosted backend for Honcho memory, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes agents. It includes an automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis, supporting any OpenAI-compatible or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory engine that uses a unified SQLite vault to persist context across different agents without cloud dependencies. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval approach—combining vector, BM25, and graph traversal—to automate decision capture and handoffs.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format for easy local storage and portability. It provides zero-cost local retrieval through hybrid BM25 and embedding search, automating memory injection for Hermes and MCP-compatible tools.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Focusing on extreme performance, Mnemosyne delivers sub-millisecond latency for memory operations using a zero-dependency SQLite architecture. Its BEAM architecture organizes data into tiered working, episodic, and scratchpad memory segments based on importance scores.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches relevant context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. Built as an MCP-native server, it utilizes SQLite-vec and local GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG capabilities for coding agent clients.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a tiered 109-entry word replacement table. It ensures Hermes's output remains human-like through a two-pass audit process compatible with Claude Code and OpenClaw.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular cross-platform library that grants agents capabilities in architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate six types of professional diagrams, including UML and ML architectures, from natural language. It includes a five-round self-check safety valve to iteratively refine visual layouts and coordinate snapping.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interoperability, this skill enables Hermes to perform cross-chain sends via CCIP and monitor multi-chain data feeds. It follows the standardized agentskills.io spec to facilitate secure smart contract interactions.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming tool allows Hermes to weave source code and prose into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between documentation and source files.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with shared memory across instances using markdown-based fabric directories. It automates the extraction of decision-quality training data to help the agent fine-tune its own replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry manages the lifecycle of reusable AI skills through an OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI. It uniquely supports skill monetization via Stripe and the TON blockchain while handling automated dependency resolution.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports structured JSON prompting and exact hex color specifications for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness for long-running tasks that utilizes structured mission planning and JSON snapshots. It enables Hermes to manage complex features through a plan-approve-execute workflow with native handoff briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints for real-time progress tracking. It ensures reliability by providing rollback procedures for safe recovery during complex operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize context token consumption. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of configurations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This forge converts repositories and developer discourse into compliant AI skills by mapping instructions to specific source code lines. it allows Hermes to generate version-pinned instruction sets for various software libraries.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing skills that features automated security scanning for prompt injection. It supports micropayment protocols for premium content and includes an agent-to-agent feedback system with trust scores.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem using the C# MCP SDK. It supports automatic skill discovery and progressive disclosure to maintain efficiency in C#-based agent environments.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This collection provides daily-updated Nix packages for a wide range of AI coding tools, including Hermes and Claude Code. It is ideal for users who need a unified proxy API and multi-agent orchestrators within a reproducible Nix-based environment.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: This security-focused deployment uses NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking to provide hardware-enforced sandboxing for Hermes. It includes over 40 integrated tools and pre-configured security policies that range from strict inference to permissive system access.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services with a focus on reproducibility. It features secure secrets handling via sops-nix and supports a hybrid skill architecture for both managed and interactive installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It integrates local model management, GPU monitoring, and a workflow engine for scheduled automation directly on the desktop.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker-based sandbox that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this headless deployment configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, allowing the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs and integrate with Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates with Portainer to deploy Hermes alongside a ttyd web terminal for browser-based interaction. It simplifies the deployment lifecycle by using GitHub Actions and GHCR for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users requiring strict oversight, featuring the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off and integrated spend monitoring.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with support for over 20 model providers and specialized features like scheduled tasks and memory. It distinguishes itself by providing visual organizational charts to manage agent delegations and team hierarchies effectively.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine harness code and improve benchmark performance. It orchestrates Hermes Agent through structured candidate mutations to track and push the performance frontiers of TBLite and TB2 evaluations.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and live scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases ranging from research to infrastructure. It ensures high-standard outputs by implementing mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing across multiple LLM providers.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by combining Hermes Agent with Supermodel code graphs. It enforces architectural integrity through an eight-phase process where each stage is protected by automated graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This tool utilizes ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution. It prioritizes execution safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits for every delegated workflow.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It utilizes LiteLLM pricing data and 3D visualizations to provide developers with granular oversight of their model expenditure.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based themes that modify colors, ASCII art, and spinner animations. It supports partial skin inheritance and includes a library of presets ranging from retro DOS aesthetics to sci-fi themes like Skynet.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, enabling native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically enhances the environment with MLOps capabilities for vLLM inference, GGUF management, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control, exposing structured page schemas and element visibility through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features durable session checkpoints and a supervisory sidebar, allowing developers to audit and intervene in automated web workflows.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for deploying scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders with local configurations and memories. It enables seamless switching between global and project-local agents, ensuring that tools and instructions remain relevant to the immediate workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter optimizes agent performance by identifying structural language bugs in system prompts and tool descriptions using seven specialized detectors. It operates with zero latency and no LLM calls, providing an automated quality gate for Hermes configurations within CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by automatically mapping personas, memories, and workspace files. It handles the complex conversion of model configurations and credentials into the Hermes YAML format to preserve existing agent setups.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script automates the setup of a full Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, facilitating a zero-cost entry point using free model routing.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI through a streamlined three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management. It offers a lightweight, mobile-friendly way to interact with the agent using only Python and vanilla JavaScript.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-channel orchestration, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp. It features advanced real-time analytics for token tracking and cost estimation alongside an integrated web terminal for backend management.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This security-hardened workspace emphasizes agent orchestration through a dedicated memory browser and skill manager. It utilizes real-time SSE streaming for chat and tool execution, incorporating mandatory approval prompts and authentication middleware for safer operations.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: Acting as a multi-agent gateway, this self-hosted dashboard allows users to start, stop, and configure multiple agent profiles from a single interface. It provides detailed token analytics and a browser-based terminal to monitor system metrics and cron jobs.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that establishes direct SSH connections to Hermes without requiring a middle-layer API or remote daemon. It features a multi-tabbed terminal and specialized tools for managing remote SQLite session stores and environment variables.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: Designed as a mobile-first React SPA for the Telegram Mini App ecosystem, this interface provides a terminal-style chat with slash command support. It allows users to spawn background agents and monitor live system health gauges for CPU and memory usage directly from their phone.
entries.Web3CZ/Web3Hermes: This localized Chinese interface offers a tailored experience for regional users with full support for dark and light themes. It includes a robust workspace file browser for direct editing and a comprehensive session management system with tool call visualization.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app that simplifies the initial setup of Hermes Agent through guided installation and dependency resolution. It serves as a configuration hub for connecting various LLM providers and messaging gateways like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: This dashboard focuses on process monitoring and historical inspection, featuring FTS5 search capabilities for deep conversation history analysis. It provides a read-only view of configurations and tracks cost distribution across active sessions.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that integrates a marketplace for skills, MCP servers, and plugins to extend agent capabilities. It utilizes isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate for different workflows.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a glassmorphic design, this single-file HTML interface uses a React-based architecture to provide live previews of HTML, SVG, and PDF artifacts. It includes a built-in memory inspector and file browser for managing local agent data.
